top_opportunities.title: [PAID BOUNTY - $750] Audio Note Recording
top_opportunities.body_preview: ## Bounty Overview Build full audio-note recording, playback, transcription, and management support for the warpSpeed Notes experience. This bounty allows users to record quick voice notes directly inside the app, save them as audio attachments to notes, transcribe them into text, and replay/manage them later through a reliable in-note audio player. The implementation should use the provided app skeleton and OpenAPI spec, with clean separation between UI components, audio logic, backend helpers,

top_opportunities.title: [PAID BOUNTY - $660] Note Locking - Biometrics/PIN
top_opportunities.body_preview: ## Bounty Overview Build note-level locking for the warpSpeed Notes experience using biometrics, device authentication, or a user-defined PIN. This bounty gives users a private vault inside Notes. Users should be able to lock new or existing notes, clearly see which notes are locked, and unlock them only after successful authentication. Locked notes must stay hidden or obscured until unlocked, and the app should require re-authentication after app restart or after a configurable timeout. This bo

top_opportunities.title: [PAID BOUNTY - $660] Enhanced Image Preview
top_opportunities.body_preview: ## Bounty Overview Build an enhanced full-screen image preview experience for the warpSpeed app. This bounty upgrades how users view images across the app. When a user taps an image, it should open in a fast, smooth, full-screen modal/lightbox with zoom, pan, swipe navigation, and optional actions such as download, share, and delete where permissions allow. The goal is to make image viewing feel modern, responsive, and consistent across the app. This bounty is part of the warpSpeed OPEN develope

top_opportunities.source: GitHub search: is:issue state:open label:bounty label:"help wanted"
top_opportunities.title: [PAID BOUNTY - $660] Inline Image Editing
top_opportunities.body_preview: ## Bounty Overview Build an inline image editing feature for the warpSpeed app. This bounty allows users to make quick, lightweight image edits directly inside the app without exporting images to another tool. From the image preview, users should be able to open an editor, crop, rotate, adjust basic image settings, and add simple annotations. Edited images must be saved as a new version while keeping the original image intact. This bounty is part of the warpSpeed OPEN developer bounty programme.

top_opportunities.title: Explorer Bounty Umbrella: Orchestrator and Account Improvements
top_opportunities.body_preview: ## Overview This umbrella issue tracks a small set of Explorer feature improvements that may be suitable for bounty work. ## Todo ### Features * [ ] [Add a delegator list to the orchestrator detail view](https://github.com/livepeer/explorer/issues/106) * [ ] [Add account transaction history filtering](https://github.com/livepeer/explorer/issues/511) ## Retroactive Grant Notice > [!IMPORTANT] > Compensation cap: **$500** (Paid in LPT) > > Before picking this up, read the [Retroactive Grants post]

top_opportunities.title: [PAID BOUNTY - $960] Attachment Summarizer Service
top_opportunities.body_preview: ## Bounty Overview Build a Node.js attachment summarizer service that consumes email attachment events from AWS SQS, downloads attachments from Google Cloud Storage, extracts content from supported file types, and generates natural-language summaries using a self-hosted open-source LLM. This bounty is part of the warpSpeed OPEN developer bounty programme. ## Reward $960 ## Difficulty Expert ## Main Skills - Node.js - TypeScript - Prisma - AWS SQS - Google Cloud Storage - Docker - Ollama / Open-s

top_opportunities.title: [Bounty] Validate user creation payloads
reasons: blocked/risky wording: ai agent friendly, xevrion-v2, agent-playground, submitted pr
top_opportunities.body_preview: POST /users currently trusts arbitrary request bodies. A client can send a custom id and extra fields, and the API returns them in the created user response. User creation should generate ids server-side, require a valid email, normalize optional names, and reject invalid JSON shapes. Acceptance criteria: - Reject non-object JSON bodies. In - Require a valid email. - Normalize email/name values. - Ignore client-controlled id and unrelated fields. - Add regression tests for these cases. /bounty $

top_opportunities.title: [ T3 Code ] Add cross-platform copy/paste keybindings to terminal component
reasons: blocked/risky wording: ai only allowed, no humans, ai agent friendly, bounty-hunters
top_opportunities.body_preview: The terminal component `ThreadTerminalDrawer.tsx` in `t3code/apps/web/src/components/` does not support copying selected text to clipboard via keyboard shortcut on all platforms. ### Fix - Add Ctrl+Shift+C (Linux/Windows) and Cmd+C (macOS) keybindings to the xterm.js terminal instance for copying selected text - Detect the platform using navigator.platform and bind the correct shortcut - Add Ctrl+Shift+V / Cmd+V for pasting from clipboard into the terminal - Show a brief toast notification when

top_opportunities.title: [$50 BOUNTY] [Python] Add error handling to diagnostic build process
top_opportunities.body_preview: **Bounty:** $50 (LT) **Area:** build.py diagnostics **Current state:** Diagnostic build lacks comprehensive error handling **What is needed:** Add robust error handling to build diagnostics **Acceptance criteria:** - Error handling implemented - Graceful failure modes - Diagnostic logging preserved - Code follows conventions **Related PR:** https://github.com/lobster-trap/TentOfTrials/pull/284 RECENT ISSUE COMMENTS: I'd like to work on this. I have experience adding robust error handling to Pyth
